title: Troubleshooting and Optimization
content:
## DVA-C02 Domain 4: Troubleshooting and Optimization

**Weight:** ~18% of scored exam content
**Exam Level:** Associate

This domain covers CloudWatch, X-Ray, common error patterns, SDK error handling, and performance optimization for Lambda, DynamoDB, S3, and API Gateway.

---

## Amazon CloudWatch

### Key Metrics by Service

| Service | Important Metrics | NOT Included (need Agent) |
|---------|------------------|--------------------------|
| **EC2** | CPU, Network, Disk I/O, Status | Memory, Disk space, Processes |
| **Lambda** | Invocations, Duration, Errors, Throttles, ConcurrentExecutions | N/A (managed) |
| **API Gateway** | Count, Latency, 4XX, 5XX, IntegrationLatency | N/A |
| **DynamoDB** | ConsumedRCU/WCU, ThrottledRequests | N/A |
| **SQS** | MessagesVisible, AgeOfOldestMessage | N/A |

### CloudWatch Agent

- Required for EC2 memory and disk metrics
- Collects: mem_used_percent, disk_used_percent, swap, processes
- Config stored in SSM Parameter Store
- IAM role: CloudWatchAgentServerPolicy

### Embedded Metric Format (EMF)

- Structured JSON logs that auto-create CloudWatch metrics
- No PutMetricData API call needed
- Combine logs + metrics in single output
- Best for Lambda custom metrics without extra cost

### Logs Insights Queries

```sql
-- Lambda cold starts
filter @type = "REPORT"
| stats avg(@duration), max(@duration) by bin(5m)

-- Find timeouts
filter @message like /Task timed out/
| stats count(*) by bin(1h)

-- Memory utilization
filter @type = "REPORT"
| stats avg(@maxMemoryUsed/@memorySize * 100) as avgMemPct by bin(1h)
```

### Metric Filters

- Extract metrics from log patterns
- Pattern -> Custom Metric -> Alarm -> Action
- Example: Count "ERROR" occurrences -> alarm if > 10/min

### Alarms

- States: OK, ALARM, INSUFFICIENT_DATA
- Actions: SNS, Auto Scaling, EC2 actions
- Composite Alarms: AND/OR logic (reduce noise)
- Evaluation: M out of N datapoints must breach

---

## AWS X-Ray

### Key Concepts

| Concept | Description | Use Case |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **Trace** | End-to-end request path | See full journey |
| **Segment** | Work by one service | Identify slow service |
| **Subsegment** | Downstream call detail | Identify slow dependency |
| **Annotation** | Indexed key-value (searchable) | Filter by user_id, order_type |
| **Metadata** | Non-indexed (not searchable) | Store request/response body |
| **Service Map** | Visual architecture | Identify bottlenecks |
| **Sampling** | Control trace volume | Reduce cost |

### Sampling Rules

- Default: 1 request/second + 5% additional
- Custom: 100% errors, 100% /payment, 5% /health
- Priority: Lower number = higher priority

### Integration

| Service | How to Enable |
|---------|--------------|
| **Lambda** | Active Tracing (checkbox) |
| **API Gateway** | Enable on stage |
| **ECS/Fargate** | X-Ray daemon sidecar (port 2000 UDP) |
| **EC2** | Install X-Ray daemon + SDK |

### Annotations vs. Metadata

| Feature | Annotations | Metadata |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **Indexed** | Yes (searchable) | No |
| **Filterable** | Yes | No |
| **Types** | String, Number, Boolean | Any object |
| **Use case** | Filter traces | Store debug data |

### Service Map

- Shows all services + connections
- Color-coded: Green (healthy), Yellow (4XX), Red (5XX)
- Click node to see traces, latency, errors
- Identifies root cause (which service is slow/failing)

---

## Common Error Patterns

### Lambda Errors

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| **Task timed out** | Exceeds timeout (max 15 min) | Increase timeout; optimize; check downstream |
| **signal: killed** | Out of memory | Increase memory (also increases CPU) |
| **TooManyRequestsException** | Concurrency limit exceeded | Reserved concurrency; request increase |
| **AccessDeniedException** | Execution role missing permissions | Add permissions to role |
| **Cold start** | New environment initialization | Provisioned Concurrency; smaller package |
| **VPC no internet** | No NAT Gateway | Add NAT or VPC endpoints |

### API Gateway Errors

| Code | Meaning | Common Cause |
|------|---------|-------------|
| **400** | Bad Request | Malformed request |
| **403** | Forbidden | WAF, resource policy, missing API key |
| **429** | Too Many Requests | Throttled (account/stage/usage plan) |
| **502** | Bad Gateway | Lambda response format wrong (proxy mode) |
| **504** | Gateway Timeout | Backend > 29 seconds (hard limit) |

**Key:** 502 + Lambda Proxy = missing statusCode/headers/body in response.

### DynamoDB Errors

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| **ProvisionedThroughputExceededException** | Exceeded RCU/WCU or hot partition | On-Demand mode; better key design; DAX |
| **ValidationException (> 400 KB)** | Item too large | Store in S3; keep pointer in DDB |
| **ConditionalCheckFailedException** | Condition not met (optimistic locking) | Retry with fresh read |

### SQS Issues

| Issue | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| **Message reappears** | Visibility timeout too short | Increase timeout > processing time |
| **Duplicates** | Standard queue (at-least-once) | FIFO queue or idempotent processing |
| **Messages in DLQ** | Failed maxReceiveCount times | Fix consumer; redrive messages |
| **Batch retry on single failure** | No partial batch reporting | Enable ReportBatchItemFailures |

### S3 Errors

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| **403 Access Denied** | Bucket policy, IAM, KMS key, VPC endpoint | Check all access layers |
| **KMS throttling** | High throughput + SSE-KMS | S3 Bucket Keys; quota increase; SSE-S3 |
| **Slow uploads** | Large files over distance | Multipart upload; Transfer Acceleration |

---

## SDK Error Handling

### Retryable vs. Non-Retryable

| Retryable (SDK retries) | Non-Retryable (fix your code) |
|------------------------|------------------------------|
| 500, 502, 503, 504 | 400 Bad Request |
| 429 Throttling | 401 Unauthorized |
| Network errors | 403 Forbidden |
| RequestTimeout | 404 Not Found |

### Retry Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| **Standard** | Exponential backoff + jitter + token bucket |
| **Adaptive** | Standard + client-side rate limiting |
| **Legacy** | Simple exponential backoff |

### Exponential Backoff with Jitter

```
wait = min(cap, base x 2^attempt) + random_jitter
Example: 100ms, 200ms, 400ms, 800ms... capped at 20s
```

---

## Performance Optimization

### Lambda Optimization

| Technique | Impact |
|-----------|--------|
| **Provisioned Concurrency** | Eliminates cold starts |
| **Increase memory** | More CPU = faster execution |
| **Init outside handler** | Reuse SDK clients, connections |
| **Smaller package** | Faster cold start |
| **Lambda Layers** | Shared deps (cached separately) |
| **ARM64 (Graviton2)** | 20% cheaper, often faster |
| **Avoid VPC unless needed** | ENI creation adds latency |

### DynamoDB Optimization

| Technique | Use Case |
|-----------|----------|
| **DAX** | Microsecond read cache (DynamoDB-specific) |
| **Eventually Consistent** | 2x throughput (default) |
| **Query over Scan** | Always prefer (by partition key) |
| **GSI** | Alternative access patterns |
| **Projection Expression** | Return only needed attributes |
| **On-Demand Mode** | Auto-scales (no planning) |
| **Batch operations** | Reduce API calls |

### API Gateway Optimization

| Technique | Impact |
|-----------|--------|
| **Stage caching** | Reduce backend calls (TTL 0-3600s) |
| **HTTP API** | Lower latency + cheaper than REST |
| **Edge-optimized** | CloudFront for global clients |
| **Throttling** | Protect backend from overload |

### S3 Optimization

| Technique | Use Case |
|-----------|----------|
| **Multipart Upload** | Large files (> 100 MB) |
| **Transfer Acceleration** | Long-distance uploads |
| **CloudFront** | Cache reads at edge |
| **Byte-Range Fetches** | Parallel partial downloads |
| **S3 Select** | Query subset of object |
| **Prefix distribution** | 3,500 PUT / 5,500 GET per prefix/sec |

### Caching Layers

```
Layer 1: Client-side (browser cache, local storage)
Layer 2: CDN (CloudFront - edge caching)
Layer 3: API Gateway Cache (per-stage, TTL)
Layer 4: Application Cache (ElastiCache Redis/Memcached)
Layer 5: Database Cache (DAX for DynamoDB)

Decision:
- Static content -> CloudFront
- API responses -> API Gateway Cache
- DynamoDB reads -> DAX
- Complex app data -> ElastiCache Redis
- Sessions -> ElastiCache Redis or DynamoDB
```

### ElastiCache Patterns

| Pattern | How It Works | Trade-off |
|---------|-------------|-----------|
| **Lazy Loading** | Cache on read miss | Stale data possible |
| **Write-Through** | Cache on every write | Always fresh; slower writes |
| **TTL** | Expire after time | Balance freshness vs. load |

---

## Key Exam Patterns

**CloudWatch:**
- **"EC2 memory metrics"** -> CloudWatch Agent
- **"Custom metrics without API calls"** -> EMF
- **"Alert on error rate"** -> Metric Filter -> Alarm -> SNS
- **"Analyze logs"** -> Logs Insights
- **"High-resolution metrics"** -> Custom metric (StorageResolution: 1)

**X-Ray:**
- **"Identify bottleneck"** -> Service Map
- **"Find slow dependency"** -> Trace subsegments
- **"Search by user ID"** -> Annotations (indexed)
- **"Reduce tracing cost"** -> Sampling rules
- **"ECS tracing"** -> X-Ray daemon sidecar (port 2000)

**Lambda:**
- **"Task timed out"** -> Increase timeout; check X-Ray
- **"signal: killed"** -> Increase memory
- **"Throttled"** -> Reserved/provisioned concurrency
- **"Cold start"** -> Provisioned Concurrency
- **"VPC no internet"** -> NAT Gateway or VPC endpoints

**API Gateway:**
- **"502"** -> Lambda response format wrong
- **"504"** -> Backend > 29 seconds
- **"429"** -> Throttled (check limits)

**DynamoDB:**
- **"Throttled"** -> Hot partition or insufficient capacity
- **"Slow reads"** -> DAX or eventually consistent
- **"Expensive scan"** -> Redesign to use Query + GSI

**SDK:**
- **"Throttling errors"** -> Exponential backoff (SDK handles automatically)
- **"Which errors retry?"** -> 5XX and 429 (not 4XX)

---

## Quick Exam Tips

- **CloudWatch Agent** = memory + disk on EC2. Not available by default.
- **EMF** = metrics from structured logs. No PutMetricData needed. Best for Lambda.
- **X-Ray Annotations** = indexed, searchable. **Metadata** = not searchable.
- **X-Ray sampling** default: 1/sec + 5%. Custom rules for cost control.
- **Lambda REPORT line** shows: Duration, Billed Duration, Memory Size, Max Memory Used.
- **API Gateway 29-second hard limit.** If backend is slower, get 504.
- **502 in Lambda Proxy** = response format wrong (need statusCode + headers + body).
- **DynamoDB hot partition** = most common throttling cause. Fix: better key design.
- **DAX** = DynamoDB-specific cache (microseconds). **ElastiCache** = general purpose.
- **SDK retries 5XX and 429 automatically.** Does NOT retry 4XX (client errors).
- **Exponential backoff with jitter** prevents thundering herd on retries.
- **SQS visibility timeout** must be > Lambda timeout. Otherwise duplicates.
- **ReportBatchItemFailures** = only retry failed messages (not entire batch).
- **S3 Bucket Keys** reduce KMS API calls by 99% (for SSE-KMS at scale).
- **CloudFront** for read optimization. **Transfer Acceleration** for upload optimization.

---

## Summary

This covers all objectives in DVA-C02 Domain 4:

- **CloudWatch** - Metrics, Agent, EMF, Logs Insights, Metric Filters, Alarms
- **X-Ray** - Traces, segments, annotations, sampling, service map, integration
- **Lambda errors** - Timeout, OOM, throttling, cold starts, VPC issues
- **API Gateway errors** - 502, 504, 429, 403 and their causes
- **DynamoDB errors** - Throttling, hot partitions, conditional failures
- **SQS/Kinesis errors** - Visibility timeout, duplicates, DLQ, iterator age
- **SDK patterns** - Retry modes, exponential backoff, error handling
- **Optimization** - Lambda (memory, concurrency), DynamoDB (DAX, keys), S3 (multipart, CDN)
- **Caching** - CloudFront, API Gateway, ElastiCache, DAX (when to use each)

---

## Additional Exam Patterns (Continued)

**DynamoDB:**
- **"ProvisionedThroughputExceededException"** -> Hot partition or insufficient capacity -> On-Demand or redesign key
- **"Identify hot partition key"** -> CloudWatch Contributor Insights
- **"Slow reads on large table"** -> Query (not Scan), GSI, or DAX
- **"Item too large"** -> 400 KB limit; store in S3, pointer in DDB
- **"ConditionalCheckFailedException"** -> Optimistic locking conflict; retry with fresh read
- **"GSI throttling base table"** -> Increase GSI WCU or On-Demand
- **"Microsecond read latency"** -> DAX
- **"RCU calculation"** -> ceil(item_size/4KB) x reads/sec

**S3:**
- **"Access Denied with SSE-KMS"** -> KMS key policy must allow Decrypt
- **"Slow large upload"** -> Multipart upload (parallel parts)
- **"High request rate throttling"** -> Spread across prefixes (3,500 PUT / 5,500 GET per prefix)
- **"KMS throttling with S3"** -> S3 Bucket Keys (reduces calls 99%)
- **"CORS error"** -> Configure CORS rules on bucket
- **"Reduce transfer for large objects"** -> S3 Select or byte-range fetches

**SQS:**
- **"Messages processed multiple times"** -> Visibility timeout too short or use FIFO
- **"Messages in DLQ"** -> Consumer failing; inspect, fix, redrive
- **"Lambda partial batch failure"** -> Enable ReportBatchItemFailures
- **"Recommended visibility timeout"** -> 6x Lambda timeout
- **"FIFO throughput too low"** -> More MessageGroupIds for parallelism

**Kinesis:**
- **"IteratorAge increasing"** -> Consumer behind; add shards, increase batch, Enhanced Fan-Out
- **"Read throughput exceeded"** -> Enhanced Fan-Out (dedicated 2 MB/sec per consumer)
- **"Hot shard"** -> High-cardinality partition key or random suffix
- **"Lambda failures blocking stream"** -> Bisect on error + max retry + on-failure destination

**SDK:**
- **"Handle throttling"** -> Exponential backoff with jitter (SDK automatic)
- **"SDK keeps failing"** -> Increase max retries or adaptive mode
- **"4XX not retried"** -> Correct; 4XX (except 429) are client errors
- **"Credential errors"** -> Check provider chain: env vars -> credentials file -> instance profile

**Caching:**
- **"Reduce DynamoDB read costs"** -> DAX (microsecond) or ElastiCache (app-level)
- **"Cache API responses"** -> API Gateway caching (stage-level, TTL)
- **"Cache static content globally"** -> CloudFront
- **"Session management"** -> ElastiCache Redis or DynamoDB with TTL
- **"Lazy Loading vs. Write-Through"** -> Lazy = cache on miss (stale possible); Write-Through = cache on write (always fresh)

**General Optimization:**
- **"Reduce cold starts"** -> Provisioned Concurrency, smaller package, init outside handler
- **"Find optimal Lambda memory"** -> Lambda Power Tuning tool
- **"ARM64 vs. x86"** -> Graviton2: 20% cheaper, often faster
- **"Async processing"** -> SQS/SNS + Lambda (decouple, retry, scale)
- **"Reduce API Gateway latency"** -> HTTP API or enable caching

---

## Additional Quick Tips

- **Logs Insights** = ad-hoc log analysis. Know: fields, filter, stats, sort, limit.
- **X-Ray Annotations vs. Metadata** = guaranteed exam question. Annotations = searchable.
- **X-Ray Sampling** controls cost. Default: 1/sec + 5%.
- **Lambda REPORT line** shows: Duration, Billed Duration, Memory Size, Max Memory Used, Init Duration.
- **502 from API Gateway** = Lambda response format wrong (need statusCode + headers + body).
- **29-second hard limit** on API Gateway. Use async patterns for longer tasks.
- **DAX vs. ElastiCache:** DAX = DynamoDB-specific (drop-in). ElastiCache = general purpose (code changes).
- **Exponential backoff with jitter** = correct retry strategy. SDK does it automatically.
- **CloudWatch Agent** = EC2 memory + disk. Tested repeatedly.
- **Contributor Insights** identifies hot partition keys in DynamoDB.
- **ReportBatchItemFailures** = only retry failed messages (not entire batch).
- **Enhanced Fan-Out** = dedicated 2 MB/sec per Kinesis consumer.
- **CloudTrail** has ~15 min delay. For real-time -> EventBridge.
- **S3 Bucket Keys** reduce KMS calls by 99% for SSE-KMS at scale.

---

## Domain 4 Concept Map

```
TROUBLESHOOTING AND OPTIMIZATION
|
+-- CLOUDWATCH (Monitoring)
|   +-- Metrics: Default + Custom + EMF
|   +-- Logs: Groups -> Streams -> Insights queries
|   +-- Metric Filters: Extract metrics from log patterns
|   +-- Subscription Filters: Stream to Lambda/ES/Firehose
|   +-- Alarms: Threshold -> SNS/Auto Scaling/EC2
|   +-- Agent: Memory, disk, custom logs (EC2)
|   +-- Contributor Insights: Top-N contributors (hot keys)
|   +-- EMF: Structured logs -> automatic metrics
|
+-- X-RAY (Distributed Tracing)
|   +-- Concepts: Trace -> Segment -> Subsegment
|   +-- Annotations: Indexed, searchable (filter traces)
|   +-- Metadata: Not indexed (store large data)
|   +-- Service Map: Visual architecture + latency + errors
|   +-- Sampling: Reservoir + Rate (control cost)
|   +-- Integration: Lambda, ECS (sidecar), API GW
|   +-- SDK: patch_all() auto-instruments
|
+-- ERROR PATTERNS
|   +-- Lambda: Timeout, OOM, Throttle, Cold Start, Permission
|   +-- API Gateway: 502 (bad response), 504 (timeout), 429 (throttle)
|   +-- DynamoDB: Throttle (hot partition), Item too large, Conditional fail
|   +-- S3: 403 (permissions/KMS), CORS, KMS throttle
|   +-- SQS: Visibility timeout, Duplicates, DLQ, Partial batch
|   +-- Kinesis: Throughput exceeded, Iterator age, Hot shard
|   +-- ECS: CannotPullContainer, OOM killed, Health check fail
|
+-- SDK ERROR HANDLING
|   +-- Retryable: 5XX, 429, timeouts, network errors
|   +-- Non-Retryable: 4XX (except 429), validation errors
|   +-- Retry Modes: Standard, Adaptive, Legacy
|   +-- Backoff: Exponential with jitter (SDK automatic)
|
+-- PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION
|   +-- Lambda: Provisioned Concurrency, memory, init outside handler
|   +-- DynamoDB: DAX, Query over Scan, GSI, On-Demand, batch
|   +-- API Gateway: Caching, HTTP API, throttling
|   +-- S3: Multipart, Transfer Acceleration, CloudFront, S3 Select
|   +-- General: Async patterns, connection reuse, ARM64
|
+-- CACHING LAYERS
|   +-- Client-side: Browser cache, local storage
|   +-- CDN: CloudFront (edge, static + dynamic)
|   +-- API: API Gateway cache (per-stage, TTL)
|   +-- Application: ElastiCache Redis/Memcached
|   +-- Database: DAX (DynamoDB-specific, microsecond)
|
+-- DEPLOYMENT DEBUGGING
|   +-- CloudFormation: CREATE_FAILED, ROLLBACK_FAILED, drift
|   +-- CodeDeploy: Hook failures, agent issues, alarm rollback
|   +-- Elastic Beanstalk: Health RED, .ebextensions, logs
|   +-- Lambda: Package too large, permissions, VPC connectivity
|
+-- AUDIT AND FORENSICS
    +-- CloudTrail: API call history (who/what/when)
    +-- Event Types: Management (default), Data (opt-in), Insights
    +-- Integration: S3, CloudWatch Logs, EventBridge, Athena
```

---

## Summary Table

| Topic | Key Services | Critical Concepts |
|-------|-------------|------------------|
| **Monitoring** | CloudWatch | Metrics (default/custom/EMF), Logs Insights, Alarms, Agent, Contributor Insights |
| **Tracing** | X-Ray | Annotations (indexed) vs. Metadata, Service Map, Sampling, SDK patch_all |
| **Lambda Issues** | Lambda, CloudWatch | Timeout, OOM, Throttle, Cold Start (Provisioned Concurrency) |
| **API GW Issues** | API Gateway | 502 (malformed response), 504 (29s limit), 429 (throttle), caching |
| **DynamoDB Issues** | DynamoDB, DAX | Hot partition, Contributor Insights, RCU/WCU calc, DAX for reads |
| **S3 Issues** | S3, KMS | 403 (KMS key policy), Bucket Keys, multipart, CORS |
| **Queue Issues** | SQS, Kinesis | Visibility timeout (6x Lambda), ReportBatchItemFailures, Enhanced Fan-Out |
| **SDK** | AWS SDK | Exponential backoff + jitter, retry modes, retryable vs. non-retryable |
| **Caching** | CloudFront, ElastiCache, DAX | Lazy Loading vs. Write-Through, TTL, DAX vs. ElastiCache |
| **Audit** | CloudTrail | Management vs. Data events, 15-min delay, EventBridge for real-time |

---

## DVA-C02 Complete Domain Summary

| Domain | Weight | Core Focus |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| **1. Development with AWS Services** | 32% | Lambda, API GW, DynamoDB, S3, SQS, SNS, Step Functions |
| **2. Security** | 26% | IAM, Cognito, KMS, Secrets Manager, VPC endpoints, WAF |
| **3. Deployment** | 24% | CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, CloudFormation, SAM, Beanstalk |
| **4. Troubleshooting & Optimization** | 18% | CloudWatch, X-Ray, error patterns, caching, performance tuning |
